reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone in headless Chrome across q8 AND fp32; on-page first-visit Download → real audio, reload auto-inits with no button; desktop+mobile layout checked at 0px overflow), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (CDP harness, fresh cache-absent profile), WASM. Standalone probe: pipeline('text-to-speech','Xenova/mms-tts-vie',{dtype:'q8'}) on 'Xin chào bạn. Giọng nói này được tạo trong trình duyệt.' tokenized to 107 input ids (add_blank-interleaved, tone-marked chars in-vocab) and returned a real Float32Array — 67840 samples @ 16000 Hz (4.24 s), peak amplitude 0.8688, mean |amp| 0.0435, 96.9% non-zero (non-silent). fp32 corroborated identically (70144 samples, peak 0.8629). IMPORTANT measurement note: an earlier sequential probe showed vie aborting — that was a pipeline artifact (a prior Korean-checkpoint WASM abort in the SAME page context corrupts the shared ORT runtime); re-tested in a FRESH page context, vie q8 and fp32 both run cleanly.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = { audio: Float32Array (mono), sampling_rate: 16000 }. The probe and the page both returned exactly that shape; the page encodes the Float32 PCM to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio> and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent Vietnamese speech (peak 0.87).
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT-language VITS demo: the Vietnamese checkpoint (own Vietnamese char vocab of 95 symbols INCLUDING the full tone-marked set à/á/ả/ã/ạ/ê/ô/ơ/ư/đ/… + Vietnamese-trained weights), materially different from the built English/German/Spanish/French/Arabic mms-tts demos.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mms-tts-vie, linked to its card), the original (facebook/mms-tts-vie), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx — verified non-degenerate), size (~38 MB) and license (CC-BY-NC 4.0). Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial) noted in at-a-glance AND the footer byline. q8 was chosen over fp32 after verifying it is non-degenerate (peak 0.869, 96.9% non-zero) — smaller download, matches the German/Spanish sibling demos.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture. Verified on-page: first visit (fresh profile) showed state='download-required' with a Download button; clicking it downloaded + validated the ~38 MB model and reached state='ready', and pressing Nói produced real Vietnamese audio. A reload takes the auto-init path — state reaches 'ready' with NO Download button rendered.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/mms-tts-vie so the ~38 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising…/Done status line reports progress. Console + network stayed clean across first-visit download and cached reload.
rubric.notes: Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: …'), not only the console.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the Vietnamese textarea, Vietnamese sample chips (Giới thiệu/Chào hỏi/Câu líu lưỡi/Thông báo/Thời tiết/Dấu thanh), the Nói button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. The practical page adds a register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains M2M100 (en→vi) + the TTS.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built English/German/Spanish/Arabic mms-tts demos.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-to-speech pipeline; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The standalone probe confirmed the exact non-silent audio the UI plays.

All buttons have accessible names; the canvas carries aria-label='Generated Vietnamese speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with ≥2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system (dark screenshot captured).

Verified 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) and clean desktop (1280×800) in headless Chrome (responsive-check + on-page measurement). The tts-grid collapses to one column ≤560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
rubric.notes: Screenshots captured at both classes, light+dark; support seeded via responsive-check + agent matrix pass.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop. Control UI stayed responsive during synthesis.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low off main thread; audio synthesis without blocking the UI
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread; keep any main-thread loop bounded.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all synthesis is in a dedicated module Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred back; the waveform draw is a bounded reduction over canvas pixels, not per-sample.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js off-thread + transfer([audio.buffer]); console clean; UI responsive during synthesis

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; logical properties; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tts-grid single-column ≤560px; audio-row/sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas inline-size:100%; readout wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile overflow measured at 360×740 in headless Chrome

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for loading progress and canvas labelling
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control; give the waveform canvas an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvas has aria-label; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: The MMS-TTS Korean sibling (Xenova/mms-tts-kor) is is_uroman:true with a Latin-only vocab, so Hangul tokenizes to 0 ids and crashes — it was recorded blocked. Is a verified client-side uroman-Korean romanizer worth building to unblock a Hangul-input Korean demo?
How much does the stochastic duration predictor vary Vietnamese prosody across sentence types (tonal, punchy vs flowing) — worth quantifying the spread distribution on the wild page.

followUpGoals.goal: Investigate a verified client-side Hangul→uroman romanizer to unblock a Korean MMS-TTS demo (currently blocked: the checkpoint needs uroman-romanized Latin input, which transformers.js does not provide).
On the Vietnamese wild page, add a duration-spread histogram across sentence types to visualise where the stochastic duration predictor samples most freely.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-vietnamese (Xenova/mms-tts-vie, text-to-speech, q8, ~38 MB — the VIETNAMESE VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; a DISTINCT-language demo vs the built English/German/Spanish/French/Arabic mms-tts: own 95-symbol Vietnamese vocab INCLUDING the full tone-marked set, so tones are preserved). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in a fresh headless-Chrome context (q8: 67840 samples @16 kHz, peak 0.869, 96.9% non-zero, non-silent; fp32 corroborated) — with a documented measurement-rigor note that an earlier apparent vie abort was a same-context contamination artifact from the Korean checkpoint's WASM abort, not a vie problem. Ladder: basics (type Vietnamese, hear it), practical (Vietnamese narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (M2M100 en→vi translate, then MMS speaks the Vietnamese). Note: MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer. Sibling note: the Korean MMS-TTS checkpoint was recorded status:blocked (uroman-only; Hangul → 0 tokens → WASM abort).
